description: Japan's aggressive push into industrial robotics in the 1990s was framed as a national necessity to address an aging workforce. Unlike Western nations, the Japanese public and labor unions generally supported the transition to avoid future labor shortages.

key_events.description: Ministry of International Trade and Industry (MITI) subsidizes robotics R&D.
Demographic reports highlight Japan's shrinking working-age population.
Major automotive manufacturers confirm no net layoffs due to robotics integration.
Public consensus firms around robotics as a 'human-compatible' labor strategy.

calibration_notes: Models the impact of demographics on the perception of automation as a positive tool rather than a threat.
